basics.name: Alessandro Barletta
basics.label: PhD Student in Mathematics
basics.summary: Incoming PhD student in Mathematics at the University of Houston. Research interests in numerical linear algebra, Krylov subspace methods, finite element methods, and numerical PDEs.

publications.name: Momentum accelerated power iterations and the restarted Lanczos method
publications.publisher: Frontiers in Applied Mathematics and Statistics
publications.summary: We compare two methods for finding extremal eigenvalues and eigenvectors: the restarted Lanczos method and momentum-accelerated power iterations, analyzing their theoretical convergence properties and introducing a preconditioning technique.

work.name: Department of Mathematics, College of Liberal Arts and Sciences, University of Florida
highlights: Implemented numerical algorithms in MATLAB and contributed to a published paper on momentum-accelerated power iterations and the restarted Lanczos method under Dr. Sara Pollock

awards.title: Cutler Scholar
awards.awarder: Department of Economics, College of Liberal Arts and Sciences, University of Florida
awards.summary: The Cutler Family Undergraduate Scholars Program provides students an opportunity to conduct supervised research one-on-one with a faculty advisor of their choosing.
